name: Candidate-oracle gap
interpretation: Best deployed closure is 0.328025; previous aggregate-policy best is 0.244220 and mean aggregate closure is 0.160601. Sample-level no-harm/margin gates were also audited; best closure is 0.082804, so margin gating is not the missing fix. Direct candidate-regret gating reaches 0.329950 unconstrained and 0.327146 under harmful-accept <= 0.25; train-selected deployment reaches 0.328025 with harmful-accept 0.251111. The selected deployment harmful-accept rate is 0.251111. Harmful-accept/ranking-penalty training is safer but weaker: train-selected closure 0.081253 with harmful-accept 0.088889. Feature perturbation improves test-sweep closure to 0.339525 unconstrained and 0.329756 under harmful-accept <= 0.25, but train-selected closure is 0.312586. A separate safety/utility head is a negative result: best closure is 0.147450, safe best is 0.090719, and train-selected closure is 0.144863. Cross-fit ensemble regret gating is also a negative result for P1 improvement: best closure is 0.287268, safe best is 0.279738, and cross-fit selected closure is 0.270989. Descriptor standardization plus group-DRO no-harm training remains insufficient: best closure is 0.110889, safe best is 0.110889, and train-selected closure is 0.093863. Joint object-set candidate gating is a negative result for P1: best closure is 0.101454, safe best is 0.101454, train-selected closure is 0.072167, and mean regret correlation is -0.010954. A regression-heavy joint gate ablation at K=16 is also negative: best closure 0.034751, train-selected closure -0.003089. Fixed-candidate/fixed-propagator downstream-loss selector training is also a negative result for P1: best closure is 0.106927, with no harmful-accept <= 0.25 deployment, and train-selected closure is 0.096833.
next_action: Move beyond post-hoc, object-set-only, and selector-loss-only gates: jointly train candidate generation, retrieval, and propagation with calibrated no-harm objectives and held-out seed selection.

name: Long-horizon state integrity
interpretation: Best WPU H=25 integrity is 0.988647; guarded sparse is 0.958508, clipped sparse is 0.201757, regularized raw sparse is 0.087153, rollout-consistency sparse is 0.084549, validity sparse is 0.084722, strong-validity sparse is 0.084722, unsafe-delta rejected sparse is 0.530270 with rejection rate 0.640000, and rollback sparse is 0.988647 with rollback rate 0.812500. Corrected rollback sparse is 0.900288 with correction rate 0.812500 and rollback rate 0.564167. Escalated corrected rollback sparse is 0.914831 with correction rate 0.710833, rollback rate 0.000000, escalation rate 0.805833, and escalation success 0.116107. Finite-clamped sparse is 0.527391 with delta norm 0.709270; finite-corrected sparse is 0.958735 with correction rate 0.784166 and rollback rate 0.000000. Selective correction preserves sparse integrity 0.958735 and improves low-disruption integrity to 0.758574 by correcting only 0.027461 of objects when correction fires, but the correction trigger rate remains 0.784166. Stride-2 and margin-1 correction gates collapse sparse integrity to 0.535190 and 0.527391, respectively. Correction-trigger frontier audit finds 0 trigger policies meeting integrity>=0.8 and correction_rate<=0.25; the best low-correction trigger is selective_corrected_entropy035 with integrity 0.653668 and correction rate 0.230000.
next_action: Selective correction lowers correction disruption but not correction trigger frequency; the next step is a learned stable transition/correction trigger that preserves integrity without correcting most sparse updates.

name: Simulator-backed benchmark
interpretation: PyBullet benchmark exists with 7 seeds and background up to N_bg=128; the 7-seed extension is still small but less seed-fragile. Coverage audit spans 9 PyBullet axes with max N_bg=2048, max total N=2052, max horizon=25, 4 mechanisms, and 7 objectification corruptions. Baseline-complete coverage reaches N_bg=256, total N=261. The N_bg=256 cup screen is baseline-complete at total N=261, but it is a low-training feasibility screen rather than a strong accuracy-superiority result. A medium-training N_bg=256 baseline-complete run raises evidence quality: best WPU `wpu-cws-indexed-local-dense` reaches accuracy 0.466667 versus best baseline `graph-transformer` at 0.450000, with WPU 60.629526x faster than that best-accuracy baseline. Baseline-incomplete large-state axes are explicitly flagged: cup_n512_wpu_only_extension.
next_action: Increase seeds, mechanisms, training scale, and long-horizon simulator rollouts.

name: Mechanism-family shift generalization
interpretation: catch_heavy: WPU 0.408730 vs baseline 0.349206; edge_shift: WPU 0.527778 vs baseline 0.571428; high_force: WPU 0.432540 vs baseline 0.460318; 3-seed calibrated mixture probe: mixture catch_heavy: WPU 0.333333 vs baseline 0.481481; mixture edge_shift: WPU 0.546297 vs baseline 0.388889; mixture high_force: WPU 0.444444 vs baseline 0.444444; 3-seed leave-family-out win-rate 0.750000: leave-family catch_heavy: WPU 0.175926 vs baseline 0.296296; leave-family edge_shift: WPU 0.509260 vs baseline 0.444445; leave-family high_force: WPU 0.500000 vs baseline 0.490741; leave-family nominal: WPU 0.462963 vs baseline 0.407407; 7-seed composition-shift stress win-rate 1.000000, mean accuracy delta 0.071428: composition edge_catch_heavy: WPU 0.412698 vs baseline 0.353175; composition edge_high_force: WPU 0.615079 vs baseline 0.611111; composition no_catch: WPU 0.599206 vs baseline 0.448413; branch-prior audit flags prior-dominated shifts: catch_heavy: majority 0.753968 vs best WPU 0.408730; 7-seed mechanism-prior adaptation changes shifted WPU win-rate 0.333333->0.666667, mean WPU accuracy change 0.138889, mean WPU ECE change 0.024819, prior-dominated shifts 1->0; prior-strength sweep best strength 0.75 reaches shifted WPU win-rate 0.666667, mean WPU accuracy 0.601852, and mean WPU ECE 0.293264; calibration-selected prior keeps shifted WPU win-rate 0.333333, mean WPU accuracy change 0.145503, mean WPU ECE change -0.046204, and prior-dominated shifts 1->0; few-shot mechanism adaptation reaches shifted WPU win-rate 1.000000, mean WPU accuracy change 0.154762, mean WPU-baseline margin change 0.050264, and mean WPU ECE change -0.055342; this is an adapted protocol, not zero-shot generalization; mechanism-aware adaptive policy reaches shifted WPU win-rate 1.000000, mean WPU accuracy change 0.198412, mean WPU-baseline margin change 0.058201, mean WPU ECE change -0.099347, and mean WPU Brier change -0.155443; this is a detect-and-adapt protocol, not zero-shot generalization; calibration-statistic shift detector recovers the adapted policy without mechanism-name routing: best-safe detector score 1.083037, shifted WPU win-rate 1.000000, mean WPU accuracy change 0.198412, mean WPU ECE change -0.099347, nominal false adaptation 0, decisions catch_heavy=selected_prior; edge_shift=fewshot_adaptation; high_force=fewshot_adaptation; nominal=base; this still uses calibration labels and adaptation samples
next_action: Train an explicit mechanism-shift detector and evaluate selective adaptation on harder held-out mechanisms.

name: Calibration and uncertainty
interpretation: Mean WPU ECE is 0.211243; mean baseline ECE is 0.219257; ratio is 0.963449. A 3-seed calibrated mixture probe gives WPU ECE 0.208404, baseline ECE 0.183805, ratio 1.133834. A 3-seed leave-family-out probe gives mean ECE ratio 0.972745. A 7-seed composition-shift stress probe gives mean ECE ratio 1.014879; worst is no_catch at 1.166073. Temperature+bias calibration changes mean ECE ratio by -0.217855 and improves 1/3 composition mechanisms. Branch-prior audit finds 1/3 shifted mechanisms are prior-dominated; worst is catch_heavy where majority accuracy 0.753968 exceeds best WPU 0.408730. Mean shifted majority-WPU gap is 0.108465. Mechanism-prior adaptation improves shifted win-rate but changes mean WPU ECE by 0.024819 and mean Brier by -0.006216; simple prior bias is therefore not calibration-safe. Prior-strength sweep finds best accuracy strength 0.75 with shifted WPU win-rate 0.666667, but no nonzero strength preserves/improves win-rate without increasing ECE; zero-strength WPU ECE is 0.263753 versus best-strength WPU ECE 0.293264. Calibration-selected prior improves shifted mean WPU accuracy by 0.145503, mean ECE by -0.046204, and mean Brier by -0.105470, but it does not improve shifted WPU-vs-baseline win-rate. Few-shot mechanism adaptation also improves shifted mean WPU ECE by -0.055342 and Brier by -0.103932, but it uses mechanism-specific parameter adaptation. A mechanism-aware adaptive policy improves shifted WPU accuracy by 0.198412, margin by 0.058201, ECE by -0.099347, and Brier by -0.155443; this is positive for detect-and-adapt calibration, not zero-shot calibration. A calibration-statistic shift detector keeps nominal false adaptation at 0 while changing shifted WPU accuracy by 0.198412, margin by 0.058201, ECE by -0.099347, and Brier by -0.155443; it reduces mechanism-name oracle usage but still depends on calibration labels and adaptation samples. Uncertainty-gated local-dense recompute improves aggregate WPU accuracy by 0.071428 and ECE by -0.016396, but it uses dense recompute rate 0.985450. The low-cost gate uses rate 0.025132, changes accuracy by 0.009260, and changes ECE by 0.005395; selective low-cost uncertainty routing is therefore not solved. A learned sparse-output benefit gate improves source low-cost accuracy by 0.052910 at recompute rate 0.205027, but changes ECE by 0.010769. Few-shot gating changes accuracy by 0.089947 and ECE by 0.041513 at rate 0.292328 (above the 0.25 recompute budget). Thus learned sparse-output routing improves accuracy, not calibration-safe low-cost routing. Calibration-cost frontier audit finds 1 non-reference calibration-safe policies under cost_proxy<=0.25. The best low-cost accuracy policy is source_learned_p0.12 with accuracy delta 0.052910, ECE delta 0.010769, and cost 0.205027. The cheapest non-reference calibration-safe policy is mechanism_selective_best_safe at cost 0.247355.
next_action: Move beyond sparse-output gates: train calibration-aware mechanism uncertainty, branch calibration loss, and multi-step ECE/Brier/NLL.

name: Systems profile and memory traffic
interpretation: Tensor-byte reduction reaches 0.997454 at mean total objects 2052.6; CPU tensorization latency reduction reaches 0.996035; random-model CPU sparse-forward latency reduction reaches 0.996975. CUDA random-model sparse-forward latency reduction reaches 0.996216 and sparse peak-memory reduction reaches 0.304080 at mean total objects 2052.4. Matched-or-better audit: N=5: matched=True speedup=0.111341; N=133: matched=True speedup=19.184067. Pareto audit places WPU on the accuracy-latency frontier at N=[133] and dominated at N=[5]. Screening-only energy proxy max is 0.999990; CUDA forward proxy max is 0.997367. Systems claim-boundary audit separates 4 supported proxy axes, 2 partial trained axes, and 1 unmeasured hardware axis. Branch-overlay memory proxy reduction reaches 0.874128. CUDA peak-memory proxy remains weak at 0.304080. Real energy and sparse-kernel behavior remain unproven.
next_action: Measure energy, allocator traffic, sparse-kernel behavior, Pareto frontiers, and trained matched-or-better speedups.

name: Objectification quality to propagation loss
interpretation: Clean score 0.957711, combined-corruption score 0.821712, combined frontier recall 0.742361. Loss-coupling audit finds worst mean accuracy drop 0.027778 for wpu-cws-indexed-local-dense/combined, worst mean MSE increase 0.087356 for wpu-cws-indexed-sparse/drop_relations_heavy. The strongest MSE predictor is selected_k_mean with |r|=0.481851; the strongest accuracy predictor is relation_confidence with |r|=0.352431.
next_action: Run stronger closed-loop or multi-horizon corruption experiments and connect objectification components to downstream loss under larger branch/rollout effects.
